name: Scylla

visual_attributes.morphology: Hybrid creature with a woman's upper body and monstrous lower body
distinctive_features: Six dog heads on each side
Beautiful woman's face up to the eyes
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Upper body of a woman with multiple dog heads and a serpentine lower body

domain_specific_visual.MYTHOLOGICAL_CREATURE: Combination of human and animal features

functional_attributes.primary_function: Devour sailors who pass too close

domain_specific_functional.MYTHOLOGICAL_ROLE: Embodiment of a natural hazard in the sea

relationships.description: Attacked Odysseus' ship and devoured six of his men
Mother of Scylla according to some sources
Father of Scylla according to some sources

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Strait of Messina, between Calabria and Sicily
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Represents unavoidable danger and perilous choices

contrastive_constraints: NOT a purely human or purely animal form
